channel.title: Writings on Joseph C
channel.description: Recent content in Writings on Joseph C

item.title: life in sections
item.description: I divide up my life into a lot of sections. Big sections, small sections - weeks, days, months, eras, periods, bits, weekends, holidays, afternoons. Self structured sections - “this is a time when I need to focus, work a job, settle into a routine for a bit.” “These next few weeks I’m able to take a break - slow down and clear my head.’ Thinking back to the Summer of 2019, maybe it was 2018, I went to Montreal for a month and worked in a hostel then the plan was to get a bus to Boston to visit my cousins.

item.title: Late night manifesto for starfish.systems
item.description: OK so a few ideas are coming together and it’s starting to take a shape. The form it’s taking is as a tiny, experimental design studio which I’ve called ‘starfish.systems’ (named after a publication I designed a year ago). How I’m going to draw a frame around this form is through a brief manifesto that I wrote late at night quickly and without overthinking it. Here it goes - 1 - build components not complete designs

item.title: Where's the slime?
item.description: Thought experiment - what if visual designs, components and websites weren’t clean with gradients, fluid animations, pixel perfect serif fonts? What if they were dirty, yucky, grew within your viewport like fungus? Images and text decayed over time. Link rot was encouraged. Sections that were separate gradually meld together to create new forms. Not the wisest idea for client work but I might start researching if any one else is exploring this area.

item.title: Narrative as a design tool
item.description: Recently I have been interested in the possibility of narrative as a design tool/research process. My story, currently with no working title, is the ‘coming-into-life’ a number of ideas, relationships, beliefs and personal experiences of mine put into a semi-coherent narrative. With writing I have found that ideas that I once thought were very much separate were in fact deeply connected while others that I felt had more important were placed in the periphery.

item.title: 2Advanced Studios
item.description: The golden age of web design 2Advanced Studios v2 flash website in 2001#WebDesignHistory pic.twitter.com/HI9YbyVGIL — Web Design Museum (@WebDesignMuseum) September 22, 2022 I saw this post from the Web Design Museum captioned ‘The golden age of web design’. It made me think of the fusion between games and digital design, there’s an energy and a coherent form. The internet arrived and provided a path forwards, code provided the means for a new aesthetic.

item.title: drawing decisions
item.description: This is a photo I took about three years ago of the Moon using a digital camera with a slow shutter speed. The camera acted as a sort of a paintbrush, where I moved the light from the Moon that came into contact with the camera lens like ink along a blank page. The shutter lasted about two seconds which I set because I wanted time to ‘draw with the Moon’ using the camera but didn’t want to let too much light in and loose the contrast of the bright moon against the night sky.

item.title: On being ok with other people being at different stages in their lives than you
item.description: Much like how light can be a particle or a wave, other people’s life trajectories can be both parallel to yours and inhabit a completely different dimension. Since reading this article called ‘Instead of your life’s purpose’, I’ve been considering a new perspective of life being non-linear — life that doesn’t move in a straight line, is not scalable and is not an easily dividable experience. Accepting this perspective has eased a lot of rigidity that I’d been feeling, days flow a bit easier now and there’s more space for meaning to shine through.
